                      W   hat is
                      dataficiation                          ?



                                                              Dataficiation                                                                        is the transformation of social
                                                              actions into quantifiable data.
                                                              This allows   for real-time tracking and
                                                              predictive analysis
                                                              This is part of a larger computational turn
                                                              that society has been under since the the
                                                              1950s (van Es and Scafer, 2017).
                                                              Dataficiation                                                                      accessing, understanding, and
                                                              monitoring people's behaviour

                       Data and Social
                       Actions



                                                                 Social media as a quantification of social
                                                                 interactions, interests, friendships,
                                                                 conversations, tastes, and online
                                                                 communication
                                                                 Quantification is made available to third
                                                                 parties – spawning   data and metadata

Dataism

“Dataism                                     pr esumes tr  ust in the objectivity of
quantified methods as well as in the independence
and  inte g rity of     institutions deploying these
methods—w hether cor   por ate platfor   ms, gover   nment
a gencies, or academic r esear cher s.”
(van Dijck, 2014,  p. 202)








                                                                                                6

Dataism                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         is not
just tr       ust in
data

It is tr  ust in a w hole data-                                               driven ecosystem, fr om
data mining cor   por ations to gover   nment data
infr astr  uctur es. T his paves the way for dataveillance






                                                                                                                                                                7

                    Dataveillance






    A combination of data and surveillance – the
    monitoring of people through metadata and big
    data
    This might happen from technology  companies or
    government
    Major issues that arise are a lack of transparency
    around what  is collected and for what  purpose
    Datafication and dataism                                                                                                                                                                                                                        as neutral paradigms
    increases a normalisation  of dataveillance,
    contributing  to an intensification  of societal power
    dynamics

Cultur   e

W  hat’s the r elationship?











                                                9

                     A brief
                     intr oduction to
                     cultur  al studies

                                                          What do we  mean by culture?
                                                          Cultural studies explores the politics, history,
                                                          and social aspects  of culture and popular
                                                          culture
                                                          ‘Culture’ as defined by Raymond Williams
                                                          as a shared system of meaning and a way
                                                          of life
                                                          What is the relationship between  ‘culture’
                                                          and ‘data’?

“Cultur e, then, is the complex and
evolving system of     meanings and values
that lies at the hear   t of     society.  It is
thr ough cultur e that we collectivel y
experience,  make sense of     and contest
pr ocesses  of     historical  change                                      –    including
lar  ge     -  scale digital tr ansfor   mations such
as datafication.”

(Bur  gess, et. al., 2022, p. 27)











                                                                                         11

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 12

Data and the
ever             yday

We’ ve been intr oduced to the ever    yday of     ‘ever    yday’
in ter   ms of     an infor   mal data liter acy, but w hat does
it mean for cultur e?






                                                                             13

                   T  he ever      yday  as
                   concept



                                                       Everyday   doesn’t mean ‘daily’
                                                       It means patterns, rhythms, routines
                                                       To understand ‘everyday’  and ‘culture’ we
                                                       are looking at everyday,  mundane, normal,
                                                       or routine practices
                                                       The relationship  between   data and the
                                                       ‘everyday’   then, is asking how  data
                                                       becomes integrated into our daily life, our
                                                       normal routines, structures, and ways  of
                                                       thinking and being







Data and Culture                                                                                                                                                                 14

                      Summar      y






                                                              Dataficiation                                                                         – a quanitifcation                                                                         of our
                                                              everyday  social actions
                                                              Dataism – an ideology  grounded in data and
                                                              data as objective fact
                                                              Dataveillance – routine and normalised
                                                              surveillance through data infrastructures
                                                              and platforms
                                                              Data and culture – data is part of our
                                                              culture, it is embedded in our systems of
                                                              meaning, ways   of understanding,  and social
                                                              norms
